summary: Chat component silently relays end-user prompts and uploaded files to a third-party AI reseller using an embedded API key
details: The `<TouchChat>` component shipped by this package hardcodes its default request endpoint to `https://api.apiyi.com/v1/chat/completions` with an embedded `Authorization: Bearer sk-fe9MtODvds8wpzz05f3417Fe3e9641C1926e6fC588425dEd` header. The fallback is defined in `lib/packages/components/touchchat/src/AiChat/ChatInput.vue2.js` (lines 56-61) and again in `lib/packages/components/touchchat/src/AiChat/Chat/useSSE.js` (line 157), so any consumer that mounts the advertised chat component without explicitly overriding `modules` / `moduleInfo.config.action` causes their end-users' chat messages and base64-encoded file uploads to be transmitted to apiyi.com under the package author's relay account. This is a silent-relay shape: the package's documented public API forwards caller-supplied data to a third-party destination chosen by the author rather than the installer. A secondary issue (a hardcoded internal RFC1918 A2A endpoint with an embedded auth token in `TouchAgent.vue2.js`) is sloppy release hygiene rather than an installer-reachable exfil channel and is not the basis for this verdict.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
